name: Cusa
description_short: CUSA FCU is a member-owned credit union serving oil and gas industry employees and their families since 1955, offering savings accounts, loans, and financial services with NCUA insurance protection.
description_long:
CUSA FCU (Credit Union Serving the Oil and Gas Industry) was established in 1955 to serve employees of the oil and gas sector and their families. As a federal credit union, it operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned basis with accounts insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) up to $250,000. The credit union has maintained this industry-specific focus for nearly 70 years while expanding its service offerings.

The institution offers a comprehensive range of financial products including dividend-bearing checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates, IRA accounts, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and mortgage lending services. Members can access online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit capabilities, bill payment services, and a vehicle finder tool for auto financing. The credit union provides additional convenience services such as wire transfers, ACH transfers, money orders, skip-a-payment options, and access to shared branching networks across the country through CU Service Centers.

CUSA FCU distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on the oil and gas industry workforce, its long-standing track record dating back to 1955, and competitive youth savings accounts offering APY rates as high as 10.00%. The institution operates with the "Once a Member, Always a Member" philosophy and provides member-centric services like skip-a-payment programs ($30 fee) allowing members to defer one loan payment annually. The credit union also publishes an annual report and maintains transparency through fee schedules available to members.

As a credit union, CUSA FCU operates under different regulatory oversight than traditional banks, with NCUA insurance rather than FDIC coverage. The website content does not provide detailed information about specific loan rates, interest rates on savings products, or extensive details about membership eligibility beyond the industry affiliation. Members should contact the institution directly for current rate information and specific product terms, as detailed pricing is not prominently displayed on the public website.
services: Savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Auto loans with vehicle finder tool
Mortgage lending and online mortgage application
Online banking and mobile banking with mobile deposit
Access to shared branching CU Service Centers nationwide
pros: NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000 on member accounts
Youth savings accounts with APY rates as high as 10.00%
Skip-a-payment program allowing members to defer one loan payment annually for $30
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit functionality
Access to nationwide shared branching network through CU Service Centers
Comprehensive financial services including auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and credit cards
Member-focused philosophy with "Once a Member, Always a Member" approach since 1955
cons: Website does not display current interest rates or APYs for most products, requiring members to contact branches directly
Appears to have limited physical branch locations based on website references to "each office" and "branch near you"
Membership eligibility appears restricted to oil and gas industry employees and families, limiting accessibility for general public
Sparse online information about loan terms, fees, and product features compared to larger financial institutions
Skip-a-payment program excludes lines of credit and VISA credit cards
best_for: Oil and gas industry employees and their family members seeking member-owned financial services
Borrowers looking for auto financing with industry-specific credit union rates and terms
Savers seeking youth accounts with competitive APY rates up to 10.00%
Members who value personal relationships and local branch access through shared service networks
diagnosis: CUSA FCU is best for oil and gas industry employees and their family members who prioritize membership with a credit union that has specialized in serving their sector for nearly 70 years. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to industry affiliation, and the website provides limited rate and fee transparency—prospective and current members must contact branches directly for current pricing on loans, savings products, and services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Cusa — Credit Union in Covington, LA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Cusa — NCUA-insured credit union in Covington, LA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
